name: Elvis Presley
bio: Elvis Presley's catalog keeps earning because it combines deep song recognition, constant film and media reuse, and unusually broad multi-generational familiarity.
notable_for: One of the most durable legacy-music catalogs in the world, with songs that continue to perform through streaming, publishing, and licensing.
career_highlight: Can't Help Falling in Love remains one of the strongest long-tail catalog standards from the rock-and-roll era.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate assumes very strong long-tail consumption, recurring licensing demand, and active estate-side monetization.

ownership.master_owner: Estate and legacy rights structures are assumed to be mixed
ownership.publishing_owner: Publishing economics depend on composition ownership, not just performer identity
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No new catalog sale adjustment is modeled here
ownership.notes: For Elvis, estate-side monetization and brand licensing are likely more important than for a typical legacy catalog.
